answer.text:
初始化:out("sem") N 次

Initialize: Repeat `out("semaphore")` for N time

For accessing semaphore, use `in("semaphore")` to acquire and `out("semaphore")` to release.

Initialization: out(sem) N time
wait: in(sem)
signal: out(sem)

out("sem") N times -> initializing the counting semaphore to N by adding n ("sem") tuples to the tuple table
in("sem") -> decrement 1 to the counting semaphore as we remove one ("sem") tuple from the tuple table, blocks if there is no ("sem") tuple present (ie waits if counting semaphore = 0)
out("sem") -> increment 1 to the counting semaphore as we add one ("sem") tuple to the tuple table

Initialisation : out("sem") N times
wait(): in("sem")
signal(): out("sem")

out(sem, 0)  # 初始化
in(sem, N)
out(sem, N)

1.Initialization: 執行out("sem")N次，產生N個許可權
2.wait(): 執行in("sem")一次拿走一個許可權
3.signal(): 執行out("sem")一次歸還一個許可權

Init: execute out("sem") N times
wait(): in("sem")
signal(): out("sem")

execution out('sem')*N
wait():in('sem')
signal():out('sem')

A counting semphore initialized to N can be implemented in Linda by putting N identical tokens into the tuple space

// Initialize semaphore to N
for i = 1 to N do
       out("sem")

// P / wait operation
in("sem")

// V / signal operation
out("sem")

Explanation:

out("sem") adds one token to the tuple space
in("sem") removes one token, if no token exists, the process blocks

So, at most N processes can pass the semaphore at the same time
Allocate memory into storage to initialize to N using the Linda coordination

initial: excute out("sem") N times
wait(): in("sem")
signal: out("sem")

1. initialization: out("sem") N times
2. wait(): in("sem")
3. signal(): out("sem")

init():execute out("sem") N times
wait():in("sem")
signal():out("sem")

question_context.prompt: Demonstrate how a counting semaphore initialized to N can be implemented using the Linda coordination language.
question_context.reference_answer:
Initialization: execute out("sem") N times.
Wait(): execute in("sem").
Signal(): execute out("sem").
